title.en: Biographies of the Marquis of Weiqi and the Marquis of Wu'an
title.raw: 《魏其武安侯列傳》 Biographies of the Marquis of Weiqi and the Marquis of Wu'an

sentences.zh: 魏其侯竇嬰者，孝文后從兄子也。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Weiqi, Dou Ying, was the son of a cousin of the empress consort of Emperor Wen.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi, Dou Ying, was a nephew of Empress Dowager Dou, consort of Emperor Wen.

sentences.zh: 父世觀津人。

translations.literal: His father's family were people of Guanjin for generations.
translations.idiomatic: His family had been natives of Guanjin for generations.

sentences.zh: 喜賓客。

translations.literal: He liked receiving guests.
translations.idiomatic: He was fond of entertaining guests.

sentences.zh: 孝文時，嬰為吳相，病免。

translations.literal: During Emperor Wen's time, Ying served as chancellor of Wu, and was relieved due to illness.
translations.idiomatic: During the reign of Emperor Wen, Dou Ying served as chancellor of the kingdom of Wu, but fell ill and was relieved of his duties.

sentences.zh: 孝景初即位，為詹事。

translations.literal: When Emperor Jing first ascended the throne, he served as Director of Ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Jing first ascended the throne, Dou Ying was appointed Director of Ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 梁孝王者，孝景弟也，其母竇太后愛之。

translations.literal: The Filial King of Liang was Emperor Jing's younger brother, and his mother Empress Dowager Dou loved him.
translations.idiomatic: The Filial King of Liang was Emperor Jing's younger brother, and their mother, Empress Dowager Dou, doted on him.

sentences.zh: 梁孝王朝，因昆弟燕飲。

translations.literal: The Filial King of Liang came to court, and the brothers held a banquet and drank.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Liang came to court, the brothers held a feast and drank together.

sentences.zh: 是時上未立太子，酒酣，從容言曰：「千秋之後傳梁王。」

translations.literal: Shi shi shang wei li tai zi, jiu han, cong rong yan yue: "Qian qiu zhi hou chuan Liang wang."
translations.idiomatic: At this time emperor not yet established crown prince, wine drunk, leisurely said: "After thousand autumns pass to Liang king."

sentences.zh: 太后驩。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager was pleased.
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager was delighted.

sentences.zh: 竇嬰引卮酒進上，曰：「天下者，高祖天下，父子相傳，此漢之約也，上何以得擅傳梁王！」

translations.literal: Dou Ying yin zhi jiu jin shang, yue: "Tian xia zhe, Gao zu tian xia, fu zi xiang chuan, ci Han zhi yue ye, shang he yi de shan chuan Liang wang!"
translations.idiomatic: Dou Ying pulled goblet wine advanced emperor, said: "The realm is Gaozu's realm, father son mutually transmit, this Han's compact, emperor how can arbitrarily pass to Liang king!"

sentences.zh: 太后由此憎竇嬰。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager from this point hated Dou Ying.
translations.idiomatic: From that moment on, the Empress Dowager bore a grudge against Dou Ying.

sentences.zh: 竇嬰亦薄其官，因病免。

translations.literal: Dou Ying also thought little of his office, and citing illness resigned.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Ying, for his part, held his position in low regard and, citing illness, resigned from office.

sentences.zh: 太后除竇嬰門籍，不得入朝請。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager removed Dou Ying from the palace gate register, so he could not enter for court audiences.
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager had Dou Ying's name struck from the palace register, barring him from attending court audiences.

sentences.zh: 孝景三年，吳楚反，上察宗室諸竇毋如竇嬰賢，乃召嬰。

translations.literal: In the third year of Emperor Jing, Wu and Chu rebelled. The Emperor examined the imperial clan and all the Dou family members, and found none as worthy as Dou Ying, so he summoned Ying.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Emperor Jing's reign, the kingdoms of Wu and Chu rose in rebellion. The Emperor surveyed the members of the imperial clan and the Dou family and, finding none as capable as Dou Ying, summoned him to court.

sentences.zh: 嬰入見，固辭謝病不足任。

translations.literal: Ying entered for an audience and firmly declined, apologizing that his illness made him unfit for duty.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou Ying came before the Emperor, he firmly declined, pleading illness and declaring himself unfit for service.

sentences.zh: 太后亦慚。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager was also ashamed.
translations.idiomatic: Even the Empress Dowager felt ashamed of how she had treated him.

sentences.zh: 於是上曰：「天下方有急，王孫寧可以讓邪？」

translations.literal: Yu shi shang yue: "Tian xia fang you ji, wang sun ning ke yi rang xie?"
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon emperor said: "Realm currently has emergency, wang grandson how can decline?"

sentences.zh: 乃拜嬰為大將軍，賜金千斤。

translations.literal: Then he appointed Ying as Grand General and bestowed a thousand catties of gold.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor then appointed Dou Ying as Grand General and bestowed upon him a thousand catties of gold.

sentences.zh: 嬰乃言袁盎、欒布諸名將賢士在家者進之。

translations.literal: Ying then recommended Yuan Ang, Luan Bu, and other famous generals and worthy scholars who were at home, advancing them to service.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Ying then recommended Yuan Ang, Luan Bu, and other renowned generals and capable scholars who had been living in retirement, bringing them forward for service.

sentences.zh: 所賜金，陳之廊廡下，軍吏過，輒令財取為用，金無入家者。

translations.literal: The gold bestowed on him he displayed under the corridors; when military officers passed, he always had them take what they needed for use. None of the gold entered his household.
translations.idiomatic: As for the gold he had received, he laid it out beneath the corridors, and whenever military officials passed by, he bade them take what they needed. Not a single piece of gold ever entered his own household.

sentences.zh: 竇嬰守滎陽，監齊趙兵。

translations.literal: Dou Ying defended Xingyang and supervised the troops of Qi and Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Ying held the defense at Xingyang and oversaw the forces of Qi and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 七國兵已盡破，封嬰為魏其侯。

translations.literal: After the armies of the seven kingdoms had been completely defeated, Ying was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Weiqi.
translations.idiomatic: Once the armies of the Seven Kingdoms had been utterly defeated, Dou Ying was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Weiqi.

sentences.zh: 諸游士賓客爭歸魏其侯。

translations.literal: Various wandering scholars and guests competed to attach themselves to the Marquis of Weiqi.
translations.idiomatic: Itinerant scholars and retainers vied with one another to enter the service of the Marquis of Weiqi.

sentences.zh: 孝景時每朝議大事，條侯、魏其侯，諸列侯莫敢與亢禮。

translations.literal: During Emperor Jing's time, whenever the court deliberated great matters, the Marquis of Tiao and the Marquis of Weiqi held such standing that none of the other marquises dared treat them as equals.
translations.idiomatic: During Emperor Jing's reign, whenever great matters were deliberated at court, the Marquis of Tiao and the Marquis of Weiqi held such authority that none of the other marquises dared treat them as equals.

sentences.zh: 孝景四年，立栗太子，使魏其侯為太子傅。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Emperor Jing, Crown Prince Li was established, and the Marquis of Weiqi was made his tutor.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Emperor Jing's reign, the Crown Prince Li was established, and the Marquis of Weiqi was appointed his tutor.

sentences.zh: 孝景七年，栗太子廢，魏其數爭不能得。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Emperor Jing, Crown Prince Li was deposed. Weiqi repeatedly contended but could not prevail.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year of Emperor Jing's reign, the Crown Prince Li was deposed. The Marquis of Weiqi protested repeatedly but could not reverse the decision.

sentences.zh: 魏其謝病，屏居藍田南山之下數月，諸賓客辯士說之，莫能來。

translations.literal: Weiqi pleaded illness, secluded himself below the southern mountains of Lantian for several months. Various guests and rhetoricians tried to persuade him, but none could bring him out.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi withdrew from court, pleading illness, and secluded himself at the foot of the southern mountains near Lantian for several months. His guests and rhetoricians tried to coax him back, but none could draw him out.

sentences.zh: 梁人高遂乃說魏其曰：「能富貴將軍者，上也；

translations.literal: Liang ren Gao Sui nai shuo Wei qi yue: "Neng fu gui jiang jun zhe, shang ye;
translations.idiomatic: Liang person Gao Sui then persuaded Weiqi: "Able make rich noble general, emperor;

sentences.zh: 能親將軍者，太后也。

translations.literal: The one who can show the general favor is the Empress Dowager.
translations.idiomatic: And the one who can bring the general close to power is the Empress Dowager.

sentences.zh: 今將軍傅太子，太子廢而不能爭；

translations.literal: Jin jiang jun fu tai zi, tai zi fei er bu neng zheng;
translations.idiomatic: Now general tutors crown prince, crown prince deposed yet cannot contend;

sentences.zh: 爭不能得，又弗能死。

translations.literal: You contended but could not prevail, and moreover could not die for it.
translations.idiomatic: You protested but could not prevail, yet you did not lay down your life for the cause.

sentences.zh: 自引謝病，擁趙女，屏閒處而不朝。

translations.literal: You withdrew claiming illness, embraced women of Zhao, secluded yourself in idle leisure, and did not attend court.
translations.idiomatic: Instead, you withdrew from court, claiming illness, keeping company with women of Zhao, secluding yourself in idle luxury, and refusing to attend court.

sentences.zh: 相提而論，是自明揚主上之過。

translations.literal: When compared and discussed, this is openly exposing the ruler's faults.
translations.idiomatic: When people weigh these matters together, it amounts to openly proclaiming the sovereign's errors.

sentences.zh: 有如兩宮螫將軍，則妻子毋類矣。」

translations.literal: If the two palaces sting the general, then wife and children will have no kin left.
translations.idiomatic: Should the Emperor and the Empress Dowager turn against you, General, your wife and children will be left with nothing."

sentences.zh: 魏其侯然之，乃遂起，朝請如故。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Weiqi agreed, then rose and resumed court audiences as before.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi took his words to heart, roused himself, and resumed attending court audiences as before.

sentences.zh: 桃侯免相，竇太后數言魏其侯。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Tao was dismissed from the chancellorship, Empress Dowager Dou repeatedly spoke of the Marquis of Weiqi.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Tao was dismissed from the chancellorship, Empress Dowager Dou repeatedly recommended the Marquis of Weiqi for the position.

sentences.zh: 孝景帝曰：「太后豈以為臣有愛，不相魏其？

translations.literal: Xiao Jing Di yue: "Tai hou qi yi wei chen you ai, bu xiang Wei qi?
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing said: "Empress dowager how consider subject have favoritism, not make chancellor Weiqi?

sentences.zh: 魏其者，沾沾自喜耳，多易。

translations.literal: Weiqi is smugly self-satisfied, fickle and rash.
translations.idiomatic: Weiqi is vain and full of self-regard, and much too fickle.

sentences.zh: 難以為相，持重。」

translations.literal: Nan yi wei xiang, chi zhong."
translations.idiomatic: Difficult to make chancellor, maintain weight."

sentences.zh: 遂不用，用建陵侯衛綰為丞相。

translations.literal: In the end he was not appointed; the Marquis of Jianling, Wei Wan, was made chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: And so Dou Ying was passed over, and the Marquis of Jianling, Wei Wan, was appointed chancellor instead.

sentences.zh: 武安侯田蚡者，孝景后同母弟也，生長陵。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wu'an, Tian Fen, was the uterine younger brother of the consort of Emperor Jing, born at Changling.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an, Tian Fen, was a younger brother of Empress Wang, consort of Emperor Jing, born of the same mother. He hailed from Changling.

sentences.zh: 魏其已為大將軍後，方盛，蚡為諸郎，未貴，往來侍酒魏其，跪起如子姓。

translations.literal: After Weiqi had become Grand General, at the height of his power, Fen served among the palace attendants, not yet ennobled, coming and going to serve wine to Weiqi, kneeling and rising like a son or nephew.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Weiqi had become Grand General and was at the height of his power, Tian Fen was still a mere palace attendant, not yet of any standing. He would come and go, serving wine to Weiqi, kneeling and rising as deferentially as a son or nephew.

sentences.zh: 及孝景晚節，蚡益貴幸，為太中大夫。

translations.literal: In the later period of Emperor Jing, Fen became increasingly ennobled and favored, and served as Grand Palace Master.
translations.idiomatic: In the later years of Emperor Jing's reign, Tian Fen grew increasingly favored at court and was appointed Grand Palace Master.

sentences.zh: 蚡辯有口，學槃盂諸書，王太后賢之。

translations.literal: Fen was eloquent and well-spoken, having studied the Panyu and other texts. Empress Dowager Wang held him in regard.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Fen was eloquent and well-spoken, having studied the Panyu and other classical texts. Empress Dowager Wang held him in high regard.

sentences.zh: 孝景崩，即日太子立，稱制，所鎮撫多有田蚡賓客計筴，蚡弟田勝，皆以太后弟，孝景後三年封蚡為武安侯，勝為周陽侯。

translations.literal: When Emperor Jing died, the Crown Prince was enthroned that same day and assumed authority. Much of the pacification and stabilization was planned by Tian Fen and his retainers. Fen's younger brother Tian Sheng—both being brothers of the Empress Dowager—were in the third year after Emperor Jing enfeoffed: Fen as the Marquis of Wu'an, Sheng as the Marquis of Zhouyang.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Jing died, the Crown Prince was enthroned that very day and assumed authority. Many of the measures taken to pacify and secure the realm were devised by Tian Fen and his retainers. Tian Fen's younger brother Tian Sheng—both being brothers of the Empress Dowager—were enfeoffed in the third year after Emperor Jing's death: Tian Fen as the Marquis of Wu'an, and Tian Sheng as the Marquis of Zhouyang.

sentences.zh: 武安侯新欲用事為相，卑下賓客，進名士家居者貴之，欲以傾魏其諸將相。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wu'an, newly eager to wield power as chancellor, humbled himself before retainers, advanced famous scholars living in retirement and elevated them, wishing to outweigh Weiqi and the other generals and chancellors.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an, now eager to seize power and become chancellor, humbled himself before his retainers, promoted celebrated scholars living in retirement and elevated their status—all with the aim of eclipsing the Marquis of Weiqi and the other generals and chancellors.

sentences.zh: 建元元年，丞相綰病免，上議置丞相、太尉。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianyuan, Chancellor Wan was dismissed due to illness, and the Emperor discussed appointing a chancellor and Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the Jianyuan era, Chancellor Wei Wan was dismissed due to illness, and the Emperor deliberated on appointing a new chancellor and Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 籍福說武安侯曰：「魏其貴久矣，天下士素歸之。

translations.literal: Ji Fu shuo Wu an hou yue: "Wei qi gui jiu, tian xia shi su gui zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Fu spoke Wuan marquis: "Weiqi noble long, realm scholars habitually attach to him.

sentences.zh: 今將軍初興，未如魏其，即上以將軍為丞相，必讓魏其。

translations.literal: Now the General has just risen, not yet equal to Weiqi. If the Emperor makes the General chancellor, he would surely yield to Weiqi.
translations.idiomatic: You have only recently risen to prominence, General, and your standing is not yet equal to Weiqi's. If the Emperor were to make you chancellor, you would surely have to yield the position to Weiqi.

sentences.zh: 魏其為丞相，將軍必為太尉。

translations.literal: With Weiqi as chancellor, the General would surely become Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: If Weiqi becomes chancellor, then you, General, will surely be appointed Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 太尉、丞相尊等耳，又有讓賢名。」

translations.literal: Tai wei, cheng xiang zun deng er, you rang xian ming."
translations.idiomatic: Commander, chancellor honor equal, moreover have yielding worthy name."

sentences.zh: 武安侯乃微言太后風上，於是乃以魏其侯為丞相，武安侯為太尉。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wu'an then subtly spoke to the Empress Dowager, who hinted the matter to the Emperor. Thereupon the Marquis of Weiqi was made chancellor and the Marquis of Wu'an Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an then dropped a subtle word to the Empress Dowager, who in turn hinted the matter to the Emperor. And so the Marquis of Weiqi was appointed chancellor and the Marquis of Wu'an was made Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 籍福賀魏其侯，因弔曰：「君侯資性喜善疾惡，方今善人譽君侯，故至丞相；

translations.literal: Ji Fu he Wei qi hou, yin diao yue: "Jun hou zi xing xi shan ji e, fang jin shan ren yu jun hou, gu zhi cheng xiang;
translations.idiomatic: Ji Fu congratulated Weiqi marquis, moreover mourned: "Marquis nature likes good hates evil, currently good people praise marquis, therefore reached chancellor;

sentences.zh: 然君侯且疾惡，惡人眾，亦且毀君侯。

translations.literal: Yet the marquis is quick to despise wickedness, and those evil people are many—they will also slander the marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Yet you, my lord, are quick to despise wickedness, and those you have antagonized are many—they will surely slander you in turn.

sentences.zh: 君侯能兼容，則幸久；

translations.literal: Jun hou neng jian rong, ze xing jiu;
translations.idiomatic: Marquis able to broadly tolerate, then fortunate long;

sentences.zh: 不能，今以毀去矣。」

translations.literal: Bu neng, jin yi hui qu."
translations.idiomatic: Cannot, now with slander depart."

sentences.zh: 魏其不聽。

translations.literal: Weiqi did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: But the Marquis of Weiqi paid no heed.

sentences.zh: 魏其、武安俱好儒術，推轂趙綰為御史大夫，王臧為郎中令。

translations.literal: Both Weiqi and Wu'an favored Confucian learning, and recommended Zhao Wan as Imperial Censor and Wang Cang as Palace Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: Both the Marquis of Weiqi and the Marquis of Wu'an favored Confucian learning. They recommended Zhao Wan for the post of Imperial Censor and Wang Cang for Palace Secretary.

sentences.zh: 迎魯申公，欲設明堂，令列侯就國，除關，以禮為服制，以興太平。

translations.literal: They welcomed Master Shen of Lu, wished to establish the Hall of Light, ordered marquises to go to their fiefs, abolished border passes, established mourning regulations according to ritual, to bring great peace.
translations.idiomatic: They welcomed Master Shen of Lu, proposed to establish the Hall of Light, ordered the marquises to take up residence in their fiefs, abolished the border passes, instituted mourning regulations according to ritual propriety—all to usher in an era of great peace.

sentences.zh: 舉適諸竇宗室毋節行者，除其屬籍。

translations.literal: They identified those among the Dou family and imperial clan who lacked integrity and removed their clan registrations.
translations.idiomatic: They sought to identify those among the Dou family and the imperial clan who lacked integrity or proper conduct, and to have their names struck from the clan registers.

sentences.zh: 時諸外家為列侯，列侯多尚公主，皆不欲就國，以故毀日至竇太后。

translations.literal: At this time many relatives of consort families were marquises, many marquises had married princesses, all did not want to go to fiefs, therefore slanders daily reached Empress Dowager Dou.
translations.idiomatic: At the time, many relatives of consort families held the rank of marquis, and many marquises had married princesses—none of them wished to leave for their fiefs. Because of this, slanderous reports about the reformers reached Empress Dowager Dou on a daily basis.

sentences.zh: 太后好黃老之言，而魏其、武安、趙綰、王臧等務隆推儒術，貶道家言，是以竇太后滋不說魏其等。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager favored the teachings of Huang-Lao, but Weiqi, Wu'an, Zhao Wan, Wang Cang, and others strove to promote Confucian arts and disparaged Daoist teachings; therefore Empress Dowager Dou became increasingly displeased with Weiqi and the others.
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager favored the teachings of Huang-Lao, while the Marquis of Weiqi, the Marquis of Wu'an, Zhao Wan, Wang Cang, and their circle strove to promote Confucian learning and disparaged Daoist doctrines. This made Empress Dowager Dou increasingly displeased with the Marquis of Weiqi and his allies.

sentences.zh: 及建元二年，御史大夫趙綰請無奏事東宮。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianyuan, Imperial Censor Zhao Wan petitioned that memorials no longer be submitted to the Eastern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of the Jianyuan era, Imperial Censor Zhao Wan petitioned that state memorials no longer be forwarded to the Eastern Palace for the Empress Dowager's approval.

sentences.zh: 竇太后大怒，乃罷逐趙綰、王臧等，而免丞相、太尉，以柏至侯許昌為丞相，武彊侯莊青翟為御史大夫。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou was greatly angered. She dismissed and banished Zhao Wan, Wang Cang, and others, and relieved the chancellor and Grand Commandant. The Marquis of Baizhi, Xu Chang, was made chancellor, and the Marquis of Wuqiang, Zhuang Qingdi, was made Imperial Censor.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Dou flew into a rage. She had Zhao Wan, Wang Cang, and their associates dismissed and banished, and stripped both the chancellor and the Grand Commandant of their posts. The Marquis of Baizhi, Xu Chang, was appointed chancellor, and the Marquis of Wuqiang, Zhuang Qingdi, was made Imperial Censor.

sentences.zh: 魏其、武安由此以侯家居。

translations.literal: Weiqi and Wu'an from this point lived at home as marquises.
translations.idiomatic: From that point on, the Marquis of Weiqi and the Marquis of Wu'an lived at home as mere marquises, without office.

sentences.zh: 武安侯雖不任職，以王太后故，親幸，數言事多效，天下吏士趨勢利者，皆去魏其歸武安，武安日益橫。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wu'an, though not holding office, was personally favored because of Empress Dowager Wang, and his frequent recommendations often proved effective. Officials and scholars throughout the realm who pursued power and profit all left Weiqi and attached themselves to Wu'an, whose arrogance grew daily.
translations.idiomatic: Although the Marquis of Wu'an held no official post, he enjoyed great favor on account of Empress Dowager Wang, and his frequent recommendations often proved effective. Officials and scholars across the realm who pursued power and profit all abandoned the Marquis of Weiqi and attached themselves to Wu'an, whose arrogance grew with each passing day.

sentences.zh: 建元六年，竇太后崩，丞相昌、御史大夫青翟坐喪事不辦，免。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Jianyuan, Empress Dowager Dou died. Chancellor Chang and Imperial Censor Qingdi were convicted for mismanaging the funeral affairs and dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of the Jianyuan era, Empress Dowager Dou died. Chancellor Xu Chang and Imperial Censor Zhuang Qingdi were found guilty of mismanaging the funeral arrangements and were dismissed.

sentences.zh: 以武安侯蚡為丞相，以大司農韓安國為御史大夫。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Wu'an, Tian Fen, was made chancellor, and the Grand Minister of Agriculture, Han Anguo, was made Imperial Censor.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an, Tian Fen, was appointed chancellor, and the Grand Minister of Agriculture, Han Anguo, was made Imperial Censor.

sentences.zh: 天下士郡諸侯愈益附武安。

translations.literal: Scholars, commandery officials, and feudal lords throughout the realm increasingly attached themselves to Wu'an.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars, commandery officials, and feudal lords throughout the realm attached themselves to Wu'an in ever greater numbers.

sentences.zh: 武安者，貌侵，生貴甚。

translations.literal: Wu'an was ill-favored in appearance, but was born into great privilege.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an was ill-favored in appearance, yet he had been born to great privilege.

sentences.zh: 又以為諸侯王多長，上初即位，富於春秋，蚡以肺腑為京師相，非痛折節以禮詘之，天下不肅。

translations.literal: Moreover, he considered that many feudal kings were older, the Emperor had just ascended while still young, and he, Tian Fen, as a close kinsman serving as chancellor in the capital, needed to humble them harshly through ritual propriety—otherwise the realm would lack order.
translations.idiomatic: He considered that many of the feudal kings were older in years, that the Emperor had only recently ascended the throne while still young, and that he, as a close kinsman serving as chancellor in the capital, would need to discipline them rigorously through the force of ritual propriety—otherwise the realm would not be brought to order.

sentences.zh: 當是時，丞相入奏事，坐語移日，所言皆聽。

translations.literal: At this time, when the chancellor entered to report matters, he would sit and converse throughout the day, and everything he said was heeded.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, when the chancellor came to court to present his memorials, he would sit and converse with the Emperor for the better part of a day, and every proposal he made was approved.

sentences.zh: 薦人或起家至二千石，權移主上。

translations.literal: Those he recommended might rise from private life to two-thousand-bushel rank; his power began to rival the sovereign's.
translations.idiomatic: Those he recommended could be elevated from private life to the rank of two-thousand-bushel officials, and his power began to rival the sovereign's own.

sentences.zh: 上乃曰：「君除吏已盡未？

translations.literal: Shang nai yue: "Jun chu li yi jin wei?
translations.idiomatic: Emperor then said: "You dismissed officials already exhausted not?

sentences.zh: 吾亦欲除吏。」

translations.literal: Wu yi yu chu li."
translations.idiomatic: I also want to dismiss officials."

sentences.zh: 嘗請考工地益宅，上怒曰：「君何不遂取武庫！」

translations.literal: Chang qing kao gong di yi zhai, shang nu yue: "Jun he bu sui qu wu ku!"
translations.idiomatic: Once requested examine work place increase residence, emperor angered: "You why not just take armory!"

sentences.zh: 是後乃退。

translations.literal: After this he then retreated.
translations.idiomatic: Only after this did he relent.

sentences.zh: 嘗召客飲，坐其兄蓋侯南鄉，自坐東鄉，以為漢相尊，不可以兄故私橈。

translations.literal: Once he summoned guests for drinking; he seated his elder brother the Marquis of Gai facing south, while he sat facing east, considering that as Han chancellor his dignity must not be privately bent on account of his brother.
translations.idiomatic: Once, when he held a banquet for guests, he seated his elder brother, the Marquis of Gai, in the south-facing position, while he himself took the east-facing seat, declaring that as chancellor of the Han, his dignity could not be compromised by private deference to an elder brother.

sentences.zh: 武安由此滋驕，治宅甲諸第。

translations.literal: Wu'an from this point grew increasingly arrogant; he built a residence that ranked first among all mansions.
translations.idiomatic: From this point on, the Marquis of Wu'an grew ever more arrogant. He built a residence that surpassed all other mansions in splendor.

sentences.zh: 田園極膏腴，而市買郡縣器物相屬於道。

translations.literal: His fields and gardens were of the richest soil, and goods purchased from commanderies and counties formed unbroken lines along the roads.
translations.idiomatic: His fields and gardens occupied the richest soil, and caravans of goods purchased from the commanderies and counties stretched in an unbroken line along the roads.

sentences.zh: 前堂羅鐘鼓，立曲旃；

translations.literal: Qian tang luo zhong gu, li qu zhan;
translations.idiomatic: Front hall displayed bells drums, stood curved banners;

sentences.zh: 後房婦女以百數。

translations.literal: The women in his rear chambers numbered in the hundreds.
translations.idiomatic: The women kept in his private quarters numbered in the hundreds.

sentences.zh: 諸侯奉金玉狗馬玩好，不可勝數。

translations.literal: The feudal lords presented gold, jade, dogs, horses, and curiosities too numerous to count.
translations.idiomatic: The gifts of gold, jade, dogs, horses, and rare curiosities presented to him by the feudal lords were too numerous to count.

sentences.zh: 魏其失竇太后，益疏不用，無勢，諸客稍稍自引而怠傲，唯灌將軍獨不失故。

translations.literal: Weiqi, having lost Empress Dowager Dou, grew increasingly estranged and without employment or influence. His retainers gradually withdrew, becoming indifferent and haughty. Only General Guan alone did not abandon the old friendship.
translations.idiomatic: After the death of Empress Dowager Dou, the Marquis of Weiqi found himself ever more marginalized, without office or influence. His retainers gradually drifted away, growing indifferent and aloof—only General Guan alone remained faithful to their old friendship.

sentences.zh: 魏其日默默不得志，而獨厚遇灌將軍。

translations.literal: Weiqi daily sat in silent frustration, unable to fulfill his ambitions, yet he alone treated General Guan with great warmth.
translations.idiomatic: Day after day, the Marquis of Weiqi sat in silent frustration, unable to fulfill his ambitions. He alone treated General Guan with warmth and generosity.

sentences.zh: 灌將軍夫者，潁陰人也。

translations.literal: General Guan Fu was a native of Yingyin.
translations.idiomatic: General Guan Fu was a native of Yingyin.

sentences.zh: 夫父張孟，嘗為潁陰侯嬰舍人，得幸，因進之至二千石，故蒙灌氏姓為灌孟。

translations.literal: Fu's father, Zhang Meng, had once served as a retainer of the Marquis of Yingyin, Guan Ying, and found favor. He was promoted to the rank of two-thousand-bushel official, and thus adopted the Guan surname, becoming Guan Meng.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu's father, originally named Zhang Meng, had once served as a retainer of the Marquis of Yingyin, Guan Ying, and won his favor. He was promoted to the rank of a two-thousand-bushel official and adopted the Guan surname, becoming known as Guan Meng.

sentences.zh: 吳楚反時，潁陰侯灌何為將軍，屬太尉，請灌孟為校尉。

translations.literal: During the Wu-Chu rebellion, the Marquis of Yingyin, Guan He, served as a general under the Grand Commandant and requested that Guan Meng be made a colonel.
translations.idiomatic: During the rebellion of Wu and Chu, the Marquis of Yingyin, Guan He, served as a general under the Grand Commandant and requested that Guan Meng be appointed colonel.

sentences.zh: 夫以千人與父俱。

translations.literal: Fu, with a thousand men, accompanied his father.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu accompanied his father with a force of a thousand men.

sentences.zh: 灌孟年老，潁陰侯彊請之，郁郁不得意，故戰常陷堅，遂死吳軍中。

translations.literal: Guan Meng was old; the Marquis of Yingyin had insisted on his appointment. Disheartened and unfulfilled, in battle he would always charge into the enemy's strongest positions, and he ultimately died fighting among the Wu army.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Meng was already old in years. The Marquis of Yingyin had insisted on his appointment, but Guan Meng was disheartened and restless. In battle, he would always charge headlong into the enemy's strongest positions, and he ultimately fell fighting in the ranks of the Wu army.

sentences.zh: 軍法，父子俱從軍，有死事，得與喪歸。

translations.literal: Under military law, when father and son both served in the army and one died in battle, the other was allowed to escort the remains home.
translations.idiomatic: Under military law, when both father and son served in the army and one died in battle, the survivor was permitted to escort the remains home.

sentences.zh: 灌夫不肯隨喪歸，奮曰：「願取吳王若將軍頭，以報父之仇。」

translations.literal: Guan fu bu ken sui sang gui, fen yue: "Yuan qu Wu wang ruo jiang jun tou, yi bao fu zhi chou."
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu not willing follow coffin return, roused: "Wish take Wu king or general head, to repay father enmity."

sentences.zh: 於是灌夫被甲持戟，募軍中壯士所善願從者數十人。

translations.literal: Thereupon Guan Fu donned armor and took up a halberd, recruiting several dozen brave soldiers from the camp who admired him and wished to follow.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon Guan Fu donned his armor, took up his halberd, and recruited several dozen brave soldiers from the camp who admired him and were willing to follow.

sentences.zh: 及出壁門，莫敢前。

translations.literal: When they reached the camp gate, none dared advance.
translations.idiomatic: But when they reached the camp gate, none of them dared go forward.

sentences.zh: 獨二人及從奴十數騎馳入吳軍，至吳將麾下，所殺傷數十人。

translations.literal: Only two men and about a dozen mounted slaves galloped into the Wu army, reaching the Wu general's standard, killing and wounding scores of the enemy.
translations.idiomatic: Only two men and about a dozen mounted slaves charged with him into the Wu lines, fighting their way to the foot of the Wu general's standard and killing or wounding scores of the enemy.

sentences.zh: 不得前，復馳還，走入漢壁，皆亡其奴，獨與一騎歸。

translations.literal: Unable to advance further, they galloped back and entered the Han camp. All the slaves had been lost; he returned with only one rider.
translations.idiomatic: Unable to press further forward, they wheeled around and galloped back to the Han lines. All the slaves had been lost along the way, and Guan Fu returned with only a single rider.

sentences.zh: 夫身中大創十餘，適有萬金良藥，故得無死。

translations.literal: Fu himself had suffered more than ten serious wounds. Fortunately there were costly medicines, and so he did not die.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu himself had suffered more than ten serious wounds, but he happened to have access to rare and costly medicines, and so he survived.

sentences.zh: 夫創少瘳，又復請將軍曰：「吾益知吳壁中曲折，請復往。」

translations.literal: Fu chuang shao chou, you fu qing jiang jun yue: "Wu yi zhi Wu bi zhong qu zhe, qing fu wang."
translations.idiomatic: Husband wounds slightly healed, moreover requested general: "I moreover know Wu wall center twists, please again go."

sentences.zh: 將軍壯義之，恐亡夫，乃言太尉，太尉乃固止之。

translations.literal: The generals admired his valor, and fearing they would lose Fu, reported to the Grand Commandant, who firmly stopped him.
translations.idiomatic: The generals admired his valor, and fearing they might lose him, reported the matter to the Grand Commandant, who firmly ordered Guan Fu to cease his sorties.

sentences.zh: 吳已破，灌夫以此名聞天下。

translations.literal: After Wu was defeated, Guan Fu became famous throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: After Wu was defeated, Guan Fu's name became renowned throughout the realm on account of his exploits.

sentences.zh: 潁陰侯言之上，上以夫為中郎將。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Yingyin spoke of him to the Emperor, who appointed Fu as General of the Gentlemen of the Household.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Yingyin commended him to the Emperor, who appointed Guan Fu as General of the Gentlemen of the Household.

sentences.zh: 數月，坐法去。

translations.literal: After several months, he was convicted of a legal offense and dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: After several months, he was dismissed for breaking the law.

sentences.zh: 後家居長安，長安中諸公莫弗稱之。

translations.literal: Afterward he lived at home in Chang'an; all the great men of Chang'an praised him.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward he lived as a private citizen in Chang'an, where all the great men of the capital spoke highly of him.

sentences.zh: 孝景時，至代相。

translations.literal: During Emperor Jing's time, he rose to chancellor of Dai.
translations.idiomatic: During Emperor Jing's reign, he rose to the post of chancellor of the kingdom of Dai.

sentences.zh: 孝景崩，今上初即位，以為淮陽天下交，勁兵處，故徙夫為淮陽太守。

translations.literal: After Emperor Jing died and the present Emperor first ascended the throne, he considered Huaiyang a strategic crossroads of the realm, a place needing strong troops, and therefore transferred Fu to serve as Governor of Huaiyang.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Jing died and the present Emperor first ascended the throne, it was judged that Huaiyang stood at a strategic crossroads of the realm, requiring a strong military presence. Guan Fu was therefore transferred to serve as Governor of Huaiyang.

sentences.zh: 建元元年，入為太仆。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianyuan, he was called to court to serve as Grand Coachman.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the Jianyuan era, he was summoned to court to serve as Grand Coachman.

sentences.zh: 二年，夫與長樂衛尉竇甫飲，輕重不得，夫醉，搏甫。

translations.literal: In the second year, Fu drank with Dou Fu, the Guard Commander of Changle Palace. A dispute of precedence arose, and Fu, drunk, struck Dou Fu.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year, Guan Fu was drinking with Dou Fu, the Guard Commander of the Changle Palace. A dispute arose over a matter of precedence, and Guan Fu, drunk, struck Dou Fu.

sentences.zh: 甫，竇太后昆弟也。

translations.literal: Dou Fu was a kinsman of Empress Dowager Dou.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Fu was a kinsman of Empress Dowager Dou.

sentences.zh: 上恐太后誅夫，徙為燕相。

translations.literal: The Emperor, fearing the Empress Dowager would execute Fu, transferred him to serve as chancellor of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor, fearing that the Empress Dowager would have Guan Fu executed, transferred him to serve as chancellor of the kingdom of Yan.

sentences.zh: 數歲，坐法去官，家居長安。

translations.literal: After several years, he was dismissed for a legal offense and resided at home in Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: After several years, he was again dismissed from office for a legal offense and retired to private life in Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 灌夫為人剛直使酒，不好面諛。

translations.literal: Guan Fu was by nature firm and upright, prone to drinking, and did not like face-to-face flattery.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu was by nature forthright and unyielding, prone to drinking, and had no tolerance for sycophancy.

sentences.zh: 貴戚諸有勢在己之右，不欲加禮，必陵之；

translations.literal: Gui qi zhu you shi zai ji zhi you, bu yu jia li, bi ling zhi;
translations.idiomatic: Noble relatives various had power at self right, not wanted add etiquette, must bully them;

sentences.zh: 諸士在己之左，愈貧賤，尤益敬，與鈞。

translations.literal: Those scholars below himself in station, the poorer and lowlier they were, the more he respected them, treating them as equals.
translations.idiomatic: The more humble and impoverished a scholar was compared to himself, the more respectfully Guan Fu treated him, as though they were equals.

sentences.zh: 稠人廣眾，薦寵下輩。

translations.literal: In large crowds and assemblies, he would recommend and favor those of lower standing.
translations.idiomatic: In large gatherings, he would single out and commend those of lower standing.

sentences.zh: 士亦以此多之。

translations.literal: Scholars also for this reason held him in high esteem.
translations.idiomatic: The scholars therefore held him in high regard.

sentences.zh: 夫不喜文學，好任俠，已然諾。

translations.literal: Fu had no fondness for literary studies, admired knight-errantry, and once he gave a promise he kept it.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu had no fondness for literary pursuits but admired the ways of the knight-errant, and once he gave his word, he always kept it.

sentences.zh: 諸所與交通，無非豪桀大猾。

translations.literal: All those he associated with were without exception great heroes and cunning rascals.
translations.idiomatic: Those he associated with were without exception men of daring—great heroes and cunning rascals alike.

sentences.zh: 家累數千萬，食客日數十百人。

translations.literal: His family had accumulated tens of millions, and daily he fed dozens to a hundred retainers.
translations.idiomatic: His family had amassed a fortune of tens of millions of cash, and each day he fed anywhere from several dozen to a hundred retainers.

sentences.zh: 陂池田園，宗族賓客為權利，橫於潁川。

translations.literal: With his reservoirs, ponds, fields, and gardens, his clansmen and retainers wielded power and influence, dominating Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: With his reservoirs, ponds, fields, and gardens, his clansmen and retainers wielded such power and influence that they lorded over the entire commandery of Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 潁川兒乃歌之曰：「潁水清，灌氏寧；

translations.literal: Ying chuan er nai ge zhi yue: "Ying shui qing, Guan shi ning;
translations.idiomatic: Yingchuan children then sang it: "Ying water clear, Guan clan peaceful;

sentences.zh: 潁水濁，灌氏族。」

translations.literal: Ying shui zhuo, Guan shi zu."
translations.idiomatic: Ying water turbid, Guan clan extinguished."

sentences.zh: 灌夫家居雖富，然失勢，卿相侍中賓客益衰。

translations.literal: Though Guan Fu was wealthy at home, he had lost his influence, and the ministers, attendants, and retainers who came to him steadily declined.
translations.idiomatic: Though Guan Fu lived in wealth, he had lost his influence. The ministers, palace attendants, and retainers who had once flocked to him steadily fell away.

sentences.zh: 及魏其侯失勢，亦欲倚灌夫引繩批根生平慕之後棄之者。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Weiqi lost power, he also wished to rely on Guan Fu to pull the ropes and strike at the roots of those who had once admired him but later abandoned him.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Weiqi likewise fell from power, he sought to rely on Guan Fu to expose and attack those who had admired him in his days of influence only to abandon him afterward.

sentences.zh: 灌夫亦倚魏其而通列侯宗室為名高。

translations.literal: Guan Fu also relied on Weiqi to associate with marquises and the imperial clan to raise his reputation.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu, for his part, relied on the Marquis of Weiqi's connections to associate with marquises and members of the imperial clan, burnishing his own reputation.

sentences.zh: 兩人相為引重，其游如父子然。

translations.literal: The two men mutually bolstered each other's standing, and their friendship was like that of father and son.
translations.idiomatic: The two men bolstered each other's standing, and their companionship was like that of a father and son.

sentences.zh: 相得驩甚，無厭，恨相知晚也。

translations.literal: They got along with great delight, without tiring, regretting only that they had known each other late.
translations.idiomatic: They delighted in each other's company without limit, regretting only that they had not met sooner.

sentences.zh: 灌夫有服，過丞相。

translations.literal: Guan Fu, being in mourning, called upon the chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu, who was in a period of mourning, paid a visit to the chancellor.

sentences.zh: 丞相從容曰：「吾欲與仲孺過魏其侯，會仲孺有服。」

translations.literal: Cheng xiang cong rong yue: "Wu yu yu Zhong ru guo Wei qi hou, hui Zhong ru you fu."
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor leisurely said: "I want with Zhongru pass Weiqi marquis, met Zhongru had mourning."

sentences.zh: 灌夫曰：「將軍乃肯幸臨況魏其侯，夫安敢以服為解！

translations.literal: Guan fu yue: "Jiang jun nai ken xing lin kuang Wei qi hou, fu an gan yi fu wei jie!
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu said: "General moreover willing fortunate arrive moreover Weiqi marquis, husband how dare with mourning as excuse!

sentences.zh: 請語魏其侯帳具，將軍旦日蚤臨。」

translations.literal: Qing yu Wei qi hou zhang ju, jiang jun dan ri zao lin."
translations.idiomatic: Please tell Weiqi marquis tent prepare, general tomorrow early arrive."

sentences.zh: 武安許諾。

translations.literal: Wu'an agreed and promised.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an agreed.

sentences.zh: 灌夫具語魏其侯如所謂武安侯。

translations.literal: Guan Fu relayed everything to the Marquis of Weiqi, exactly as he had discussed with the Marquis of Wu'an.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu reported to the Marquis of Weiqi exactly what he had said to the Marquis of Wu'an and the response he had received.

sentences.zh: 魏其與其夫人益市牛酒，夜灑埽，早帳具至旦。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Weiqi and his wife bought additional beef and wine, swept and sprinkled the grounds that night, and prepared the canopies and feast until dawn.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi and his wife purchased beef and wine, swept and sprinkled the grounds that evening, and set up the canopies and prepared the feast, working through the night until dawn.

sentences.zh: 平明，令門下候伺。

translations.literal: At daybreak, he ordered attendants at the gate to watch and wait.
translations.idiomatic: At daybreak, he stationed attendants at the gate to watch for the chancellor's arrival.

sentences.zh: 至日中，丞相不來。

translations.literal: By midday, the chancellor had not come.
translations.idiomatic: By noon, the chancellor had still not arrived.

sentences.zh: 魏其謂灌夫曰：「丞相豈忘之哉？」

translations.literal: Wei qi wei Guan fu yue: "Cheng xiang qi wang zhi zai?"
translations.idiomatic: Weiqi told Guan Fu: "Chancellor how forgot it?"

sentences.zh: 灌夫不懌，曰：「夫以服請，宜往。」

translations.literal: Guan fu bu yi, yue: "Fu yi fu qing, yi wang."
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu displeased, said: "Husband with mourning requested, should go."

sentences.zh: 乃駕，自往迎丞相。

translations.literal: He then ordered his carriage harnessed and went in person to welcome the chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: He then had his carriage harnessed and went in person to fetch the chancellor.

sentences.zh: 丞相特前戲許灌夫，殊無意往。

translations.literal: The chancellor had previously only agreed to Guan Fu in jest, and had no real intention of going.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor had only casually and jokingly agreed to Guan Fu's invitation and had no real intention of going.

sentences.zh: 及夫至門，丞相尚臥。

translations.literal: When Fu arrived at the gate, the chancellor was still lying in bed.
translations.idiomatic: When Guan Fu arrived at his gate, the chancellor was still in bed.

sentences.zh: 於是夫入見，曰：「將軍昨日幸許過魏其，魏其夫妻治具，自旦至今，未敢嘗食。」

translations.literal: Yu shi fu ru jian, yue: "Jiang jun zuo ri xing xu guo Wei qi, Wei qi fu qi zhi ju, zi dan zhi jin, wei gan chang shi."
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon husband entered saw, said: "General yesterday fortunate promised pass Weiqi, Weiqi husband wife prepared utensils, from dawn until now, not dared taste food."

sentences.zh: 武安鄂謝曰：「吾昨日醉，忽忘與仲孺言。」

translations.literal: Wu an e xie yue: "Wu zuo ri zui, hu wang yu Zhong ru yan."
translations.idiomatic: Wuan stuttered apologized: "I yesterday drunk, suddenly forgot with Zhongru spoke."

sentences.zh: 乃駕往，又徐行，灌夫愈益怒。

translations.literal: He then had his carriage harnessed and departed, yet traveled slowly. Guan Fu's anger grew increasingly.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor then had his carriage harnessed and set out, but traveled at a leisurely pace. Guan Fu's anger deepened with every passing moment.

sentences.zh: 及飲酒酣，夫起舞屬丞相，丞相不起，夫從坐上語侵之。

translations.literal: When the drinking was in full swing, Fu rose to dance and invited the chancellor to join. The chancellor did not rise. Fu, from his seat, directed insulting words at him.
translations.idiomatic: When the wine had been flowing freely, Guan Fu rose to dance and invited the chancellor to join him. The chancellor refused to rise. From his seat, Guan Fu hurled insults at him.

sentences.zh: 魏其乃扶灌夫去，謝丞相。

translations.literal: Weiqi then supported Guan Fu and left, apologizing to the chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi took Guan Fu by the arm and led him away, then apologized to the chancellor.

sentences.zh: 丞相卒飲至夜，極驩而去。

translations.literal: The chancellor continued drinking until nightfall, departing in great delight.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor stayed on drinking until nightfall and departed in fine spirits.

sentences.zh: 丞相嘗使籍福請魏其城南田。

translations.literal: The chancellor once sent Ji Fu to request from Weiqi a tract of farmland south of the city.
translations.idiomatic: On an earlier occasion, the chancellor had sent Ji Fu to request from the Marquis of Weiqi a tract of farmland south of the city.

sentences.zh: 魏其大望曰：「老僕雖棄，將軍雖貴，寧可以勢奪乎！」

translations.literal: Wei qi da wang yue: "Lao pu sui qi, jiang jun sui gui, ning ke yi shi duo hu!"
translations.idiomatic: Weiqi greatly disappointed: "Old servant though abandoned, general though noble, how can with power seize!"

sentences.zh: 不許。

translations.literal: He did not permit it.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi refused.

sentences.zh: 灌夫聞，怒，罵籍福。

translations.literal: When Guan Fu heard this, he was furious and cursed Ji Fu.
translations.idiomatic: When Guan Fu learned of this, he was furious and cursed Ji Fu for his role.

sentences.zh: 籍福惡兩人有郤，乃謾自好謝丞相曰：「魏其老且死，易忍，且待之。」

translations.literal: Ji Fu e liang ren you xi, nai man zi hao xie cheng xiang yue: "Wei qi lao qie si, yi ren, qie dai zhi."
translations.idiomatic: Ji Fu hated two people had enmity, then recklessly self good apologized chancellor: "Weiqi old moreover die, easy endure, moreover wait it."

sentences.zh: 已而武安聞魏其、灌夫實怒不予田，亦怒曰：「魏其子嘗殺人，蚡活之。

translations.literal: Yi er Wu an wen Wei qi, Guan fu shi nu bu yu tian, yi nu yue: "Wei qi zi chang sha ren, Fen huo zhi.
translations.idiomatic: After that Wuan heard Weiqi, Guan Fu truly angry not gave field, also angered: "Weiqi son once killed person, Fen saved him.

sentences.zh: 蚡事魏其無所不可，何愛數頃田？

translations.literal: Fen shi Wei qi wu suo bu ke, he ai shu qing tian?
translations.idiomatic: Fen served Weiqi nothing not acceptable, why begrudge several qing field?

sentences.zh: 且灌夫何與也？

translations.literal: Qie Guan fu he yu ye?
translations.idiomatic: Moreover Guan Fu what relation?

sentences.zh: 吾不敢複求田。」

translations.literal: Wu bu gan fu qiu tian."
translations.idiomatic: I not dare again request field."

sentences.zh: 武安由此大怨灌夫、魏其。

translations.literal: Wu'an from this bore a great grudge against Guan Fu and Weiqi.
translations.idiomatic: From that point on, the Marquis of Wu'an harbored a deep grudge against both Guan Fu and the Marquis of Weiqi.

sentences.zh: 元光四年春，丞相言灌夫家在潁川，橫甚，民苦之。

translations.literal: In the spring of the fourth year of Yuanguang, the chancellor reported that Guan Fu's family in Yingchuan was exceedingly arrogant and the people suffered under them.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the fourth year of the Yuanguang era, the chancellor reported that Guan Fu's family in Yingchuan had grown exceedingly tyrannical and that the people suffered under their oppression.

sentences.zh: 請案。

translations.literal: He requested that the matter be investigated.
translations.idiomatic: He requested a formal investigation.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「此丞相事，何請。」

translations.literal: Shang yue: "Ci cheng xiang shi, he qing."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor said: "This chancellor matter, why request."

sentences.zh: 灌夫亦持丞相陰事，為奸利，受淮南王金與語言。

translations.literal: Guan Fu also possessed knowledge of the chancellor's secret dealings—pursuing corrupt gains and accepting gold and communications from the King of Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu, for his part, held damaging knowledge of the chancellor's secret dealings—how he had pursued corrupt gains and accepted gold and confidential communications from the King of Huainan.

sentences.zh: 賓客居間，遂止，俱解。

translations.literal: Retainers mediated between them, and the matter was dropped; both sides stood down.
translations.idiomatic: Retainers intervened as mediators, and the affair was dropped—both sides stood down.

sentences.zh: 夏，丞相取燕王女為夫人，有太后詔，召列侯宗室皆往賀。

translations.literal: That summer, the chancellor took the daughter of the King of Yan as his wife. By the Empress Dowager's decree, all marquises and members of the imperial clan were summoned to offer congratulations.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, the chancellor married the daughter of the King of Yan. By decree of the Empress Dowager, all marquises and members of the imperial clan were summoned to attend and offer their congratulations.

sentences.zh: 魏其侯過灌夫，欲與俱。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Weiqi called on Guan Fu, wishing to go together.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi called on Guan Fu, hoping they might attend together.

sentences.zh: 夫謝曰：「夫數以酒失得過丞相，丞相今者又與夫有郤。」

translations.literal: Fu xie yue: "Fu shu yi jiu shi de guo cheng xiang, cheng xiang jin zhe you yu fu you xi."
translations.idiomatic: Fu apologized: "Fu several times with wine caused offense chancellor, chancellor now moreover with Fu had enmity."

sentences.zh: 魏其曰：「事已解。」

translations.literal: Wei qi yue: "Shi yi jie."
translations.idiomatic: Weiqi said: "Matter already resolved."

sentences.zh: 彊與俱。

translations.literal: He insisted and they went together.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi insisted, and they went together.

sentences.zh: 飲酒酣，武安起為壽，坐皆避席伏。

translations.literal: When the wine flowed freely, Wu'an rose to offer a longevity toast; all the guests left their seats and prostrated themselves.
translations.idiomatic: When the wine had been flowing freely, the Marquis of Wu'an rose to offer a toast to the guests' health. Every guest left his seat and prostrated himself.

sentences.zh: 已魏其侯為壽，獨故人避席耳，餘半膝席。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Weiqi rose to offer his toast, only his old acquaintances left their seats; the rest merely half-rose on their knees.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Weiqi rose to offer his toast, only his old acquaintances left their seats to bow. The rest merely half-rose or stayed where they were.

sentences.zh: 灌夫不悅。

translations.literal: Guan Fu was displeased.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu was deeply displeased.

sentences.zh: 起行酒，至武安，武安膝席曰：「不能滿觴。」

translations.literal: Qi xing jiu, zhi Wu an, Wu an xi xi yue: "Bu neng man shang."
translations.idiomatic: Rose circulated wine, reached Wuan, Wuan kneeled sat said: "Cannot fill goblet."

sentences.zh: 夫怒，因嘻笑曰：「將軍貴人也，屬之！」

translations.literal: Fu nu, yin xi xiao yue: "Jiang jun gui ren ye, shu zhi!"
translations.idiomatic: Fu angered, because laughed said: "General noble person, entrusted it!"

sentences.zh: 時武安不肯。

translations.literal: At the time Wu'an was unwilling.
translations.idiomatic: But the Marquis of Wu'an refused.

sentences.zh: 行酒次至臨汝侯，臨汝侯方與程不識耳語，又不避席。

translations.literal: The round of wine reached the Marquis of Linru, who was just then whispering with Cheng Bushi and did not leave his seat.
translations.idiomatic: When the round of toasts reached the Marquis of Linru, he was whispering with Cheng Bushi and did not bother to leave his seat.

sentences.zh: 夫無所發怒，乃罵臨汝侯曰：「生平毀程不識不直一錢，今日長者為壽，乃效女兒呫囁耳語！」

translations.literal: Fu wu suo fa nu, nai ma Lin ru hou yue: "Sheng ping hui Cheng bu shi bu zhi yi qian, jin ri zhang zhe wei shou, nai xiao nu er che nie er yu!"
translations.idiomatic: Fu nothing place vent anger, then cursed Linru marquis: "Lifetime slandered Cheng Bushi not worth one coin, today elders for longevity toast, moreover imitate girl chirping whispering!"

sentences.zh: 武安謂灌夫曰：「程李俱東西宮衛尉，今眾辱程將軍，仲孺獨不為李將軍地乎？」

translations.literal: Wu an wei Guan fu yue: "Cheng Li ju dong xi gong wei wei, jin zhong ru Cheng jiang jun, Zhong ru du bu wei Li jiang jun di hu?"
translations.idiomatic: Wuan told Guan Fu: "Cheng Li both east west palace guards, now crowd insulted Cheng general, Zhongru alone not for Li general face?"

sentences.zh: 灌夫曰：「今日斬頭陷匈，何知程李乎！」

translations.literal: Guan fu yue: "Jin ri zhan tou xian xiong, he zhi Cheng Li hu!"
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu said: "Today behead head pit chest, what know Cheng Li!"

sentences.zh: 坐乃起更衣，稍稍去。

translations.literal: The guests rose, ostensibly to change their robes, and gradually left.
translations.idiomatic: The guests began to rise, ostensibly to relieve themselves, and one by one slipped away.

sentences.zh: 魏其侯去，麾灌夫出。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Weiqi departed and beckoned Guan Fu to follow him out.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi rose to leave, beckoning Guan Fu to follow him out.

sentences.zh: 武安遂怒曰：「此吾驕灌夫罪。」

translations.literal: Wu an sui nu yue: "Ci wu jiao Guan fu zui."
translations.idiomatic: Wuan then angered: "This I arrogant Guan Fu crime."

sentences.zh: 乃令騎留灌夫。

translations.literal: He then ordered his cavalry to detain Guan Fu.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an then ordered his riders to detain Guan Fu.

sentences.zh: 灌夫欲出不得。

translations.literal: Guan Fu tried to leave but could not.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Fu tried to leave but was unable to break free.

sentences.zh: 籍福起為謝，案灌夫項令謝。

translations.literal: Ji Fu rose to apologize on his behalf, pressing down on Guan Fu's neck and ordering him to apologize.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Fu rose and attempted to apologize on Guan Fu's behalf, pushing down on his neck and ordering him to bow and apologize.

sentences.zh: 夫愈怒，不肯謝。

translations.literal: Fu grew even angrier and refused to apologize.
translations.idiomatic: But Guan Fu only grew angrier and refused to apologize.

sentences.zh: 武安乃麾騎縛夫置傳舍，召長史曰：「今日召宗室，有詔。」

translations.literal: Wu an nai hui ji fu fu zhi zhuan she, zhao chang shi yue: "Jin ri zhao zong shi, you zhao."
translations.idiomatic: Wuan then beckoned cavalry bound Fu placed relay station, summoned chancellor secretary: "Today summoned imperial clan, have decree."

sentences.zh: 劾灌夫罵坐不敬，系居室。

translations.literal: He impeached Guan Fu for cursing at the banquet in a disrespectful manner and had him arrested and confined.
translations.idiomatic: He impeached Guan Fu for cursing at the banquet and showing gross disrespect, and had him arrested and thrown into confinement.

sentences.zh: 遂按其前事，遣吏分曹逐捕諸灌氏支屬，皆得棄市罪。

translations.literal: He then investigated Guan Fu's prior offenses, dispatching officials in separate teams to pursue and arrest all the branches and relatives of the Guan clan. All were found guilty of capital crimes.
translations.idiomatic: He then dredged up Guan Fu's prior offenses, dispatching officials in separate teams to hunt down and arrest all the members and relatives of the Guan clan. Every one of them was found guilty of crimes punishable by public execution.

sentences.zh: 魏其侯大媿，為資使賓客請，莫能解。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Weiqi was deeply ashamed. He provided funds and sent retainers to plead on Guan Fu's behalf, but none could resolve the matter.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi was consumed with guilt. He spent freely and sent his retainers to plead on Guan Fu's behalf, but none could secure his release.

sentences.zh: 武安吏皆為耳目，諸灌氏皆亡匿，夫系，遂不得告言武安陰事。

translations.literal: Wu'an's officials all served as his eyes and ears. All the Guan clan members fled into hiding. With Fu imprisoned, they could not report Wu'an's secret dealings.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an's officials served as his eyes and ears everywhere. The remaining members of the Guan clan had all fled into hiding. With Guan Fu in prison, there was no one left who could expose the Marquis of Wu'an's secret dealings.

sentences.zh: 魏其銳身為救灌夫。

translations.literal: Weiqi risked himself to save Guan Fu.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi threw himself body and soul into the effort to save Guan Fu.

sentences.zh: 夫人諫魏其曰：「灌將軍得罪丞相，與太后家忤，寧可救邪？」

translations.literal: Fu ren jian Wei qi yue: "Guan jiang jun de zui cheng xiang, yu tai hou jia wu, ning ke jiu xie?"
translations.idiomatic: Wife advised Weiqi: "Guan general offended chancellor, with empress dowager family contrary, how can save?"

sentences.zh: 魏其侯曰：「侯自我得之，自我捐之，無所恨。

translations.literal: Wei qi hou yue: "Hou zi wo de zhi, zi wo juan zhi, wu suo hen.
translations.idiomatic: Weiqi marquis said: "Marquis from I obtained it, from I abandoned it, nothing regret.

sentences.zh: 且終不令灌仲孺獨死，嬰獨生。」

translations.literal: Qie zhong bu ling Guan Zhong ru du si, ying du sheng."
translations.idiomatic: Moreover finally not let Guan Zhongru alone die, Ying alone live."

sentences.zh: 乃匿其家，竊出上書。

translations.literal: He then concealed his household and secretly submitted a memorial to the throne.
translations.idiomatic: He kept his household hidden and secretly submitted a memorial to the throne.

sentences.zh: 立召入，具言灌夫醉飽事，不足誅。

translations.literal: He was immediately summoned in, and gave a full account of Guan Fu's drunken offenses, arguing they were not enough to warrant execution.
translations.idiomatic: He was immediately summoned for an audience, where he laid out the full circumstances of Guan Fu's drunken behavior, arguing that it was far from sufficient grounds for execution.

sentences.zh: 上然之，賜魏其食，曰：「東朝廷辯之。」

translations.literal: Shang ran zhi, ci Wei qi shi, yue: "Dong chao ting bian zhi."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor agreed it, granted Weiqi food, said: "East court will debate it."

sentences.zh: 魏其之東朝，盛推灌夫之善，言其醉飽得過，乃丞相以他事誣罪之。

translations.literal: At the eastern court, Weiqi vigorously praised Guan Fu's virtues, saying his offense came merely from drunkenness at a feast, and that the chancellor was using other matters to falsely accuse him.
translations.idiomatic: At the eastern court, the Marquis of Weiqi vigorously praised Guan Fu's virtues, arguing that his offenses had merely arisen from excess drinking at a feast, and that the chancellor was using unrelated matters to frame him on false charges.

sentences.zh: 武安又盛毀灌夫所為橫恣，罪逆不道。

translations.literal: Wu'an in turn vehemently denounced Guan Fu's conduct as arrogant and lawless, his crimes treasonous and unconscionable.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Wu'an in turn vehemently denounced Guan Fu's conduct as arrogant and lawless, declaring his crimes treasonous and unconscionable.

sentences.zh: 魏其度不可奈何，因言丞相短。

translations.literal: Weiqi gauged that nothing could be done, and so resorted to speaking of the chancellor's faults.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing that he could not prevail, the Marquis of Weiqi resorted to attacking the chancellor's own failings.

sentences.zh: 武安曰：「天下幸而安樂無事，蚡得為肺腑，所好音樂狗馬田宅。

translations.literal: Wu an yue: "Tian xia xing er an le wu shi, Fen de wei fei fu, suo hao yin yue gou ma tian zhai.
translations.idiomatic: Wuan said: "Realm fortunately peaceful happy without affairs, Fen got be lung liver, what liked music dogs horses fields residences.

sentences.zh: 蚡所愛倡優巧匠之屬，不如魏其、灌夫日夜招聚天下豪桀壯士與論議，腹誹而心謗，不仰視天而俯畫地，辟倪兩宮間，幸天下有變，而欲有大功。

translations.literal: What Fen loves are merely singers, entertainers, and skilled craftsmen. This is nothing compared to Weiqi and Guan Fu, who day and night gather the realm's heroes and stalwarts to plot and deliberate, harboring slander in their bellies and rebellion in their hearts, not looking up at Heaven but gazing down to trace designs on the ground, spying between the two palaces, hoping for upheaval in the realm so they might seize great glory.
translations.idiomatic: What Tian Fen enjoys is nothing more than the company of singers, entertainers, and skilled artisans. Far more troubling are the Marquis of Weiqi and Guan Fu, who day and night gather the realm's heroes and warriors to plot and deliberate, harboring slander in their bellies and rebellion in their hearts, gazing downward to trace designs upon the ground rather than looking up to Heaven, spying on the affairs between the two palaces, hoping for upheaval in the realm so they might seize great glory.

sentences.zh: 臣乃不知魏其等所為。」

translations.literal: Chen nai bu zhi Wei qi deng suo wei."
translations.idiomatic: Subject then not know Weiqi etc. conduct."

translations.idiomatic: Thereupon emperor asked court ministers: "Two people who right?"

translations.literal: Yu shi da fu Han An guo yue: "Wei qi yan Guan fu fu si shi, shen he ji chi ru bu ce zhi Wu jun, shen bei shu shi chuang, ming guan san jun, ci tian xia zhuang shi, fei you da e, zheng bei jiu, bu zu yin ta guo yi zhu ye.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial censor Han Anguo said: "Weiqi spoke Guan Fu father died in service, body carried halberd galloped entered immeasurable Wu army, body hit dozens wounds, name crowned three armies, this realm stalwart, not have great evil, contended goblet wine, insufficient cite other faults to execute.

sentences.zh: 魏其言是也。

translations.literal: Weiqi's words are correct.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi's arguments are sound.

sentences.zh: 丞相亦言灌夫通奸猾，侵細民，家累巨萬，橫恣潁川，淩轢宗室，侵犯骨肉，此所謂『枝大於本，脛大於股，不折必披』，丞相言亦是。

translations.literal: Cheng xiang yi yan Guan fu tong jian hua, qin xi min, jia lei ju wan, heng zi Ying chuan, ling li zong shi, qin fan gu rou, ci suo wei "zhi da yu ben, jing da yu gu, bu zhe bi pi", cheng xiang yan yi shi.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor moreover spoke Guan Fu connected corrupt rascals, invaded petty people, family amassed huge ten thousand, arrogant unrestrained Yingchuan, overbearing imperial clan, offended flesh blood, this called 'branch great than root, calf great than thigh, not break must split', chancellor speech also correct.

sentences.zh: 唯明主裁之。」

translations.literal: Wei ming zhu cai zhi."
translations.idiomatic: Only enlightened ruler decide it."

translations.idiomatic: The Director of Noble Titles, Ji An, sided with the Marquis of Weiqi.

sentences.zh: 內史鄭當時是魏其，後不敢堅對。

translations.literal: The Prefect of the Capital, Zheng Dangshi, sided with Weiqi, but later did not dare to hold his position firmly.
translations.idiomatic: The Prefect of the Capital, Zheng Dangshi, initially sided with the Marquis of Weiqi, but later did not dare to maintain his position.

sentences.zh: 餘皆莫敢對。

translations.literal: The rest all did not dare to speak.
translations.idiomatic: None of the rest dared to speak.

sentences.zh: 上怒內史曰：「公平生數言魏其、武安長短，今日廷論，局趣效轅下駒，吾並斬若屬矣。」

translations.literal: Shang nu nei shi yue: "Gong ping sheng shu yan Wei qi, Wu an chang duan, jin ri ting lun, ju qu xiao yuan xia ju, wu bing zhan ruo shu yi."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor angered internal affairs: "You lifetime repeatedly spoke Weiqi, Wuan long short, today court discussion, curled rushed like cart under colt, I together behead you lot."

sentences.zh: 即罷起入，上食太后。

translations.literal: When the session ended, the Emperor rose and went in to dine with the Empress Dowager.
translations.idiomatic: When the debate was adjourned, the Emperor rose and went in to dine with the Empress Dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后亦已使人候伺，具以告太后。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager had already sent people to observe and spy, and the full details had been reported to her.
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager had already dispatched people to observe the proceedings, and a full report had been delivered to her.

sentences.zh: 太后怒，不食，曰：「今我在也，而人皆藉吾弟，令我百歲後，皆魚肉之矣。

translations.literal: Tai hou nu, bu shi, yue: "Jin wo zai ye, er ren jie jie wo di, ling wo bai sui hou, jie yu rou zhi yi.
translations.idiomatic: Empress dowager angered, not ate, said: "Now I am here, but people all bully my younger brother, make me hundred years later, all fish meat him.

sentences.zh: 且帝甯能為石人邪！

translations.literal: Qie di ning neng wei shi ren xie!
translations.idiomatic: Moreover emperor how can be stone person!

sentences.zh: 此特帝在，即錄錄，設百歲後，是屬寧有可信者乎？」

translations.literal: Ci te di zai, ji lu lu, she bai sui hou, shi shu ning you ke xin zhe hu?"
translations.idiomatic: This especially emperor here, hesitant hesitant, if hundred years later, these people how have trustworthy ones?"

sentences.zh: 上謝曰：「俱宗室外家，故廷辯之。

translations.literal: Shang xie yue: "Ju zong shi wai jia, gu ting bian zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor apologized: "Both imperial clan maternal relatives, therefore court debated it.

sentences.zh: 不然，此一獄吏所決耳。」

translations.literal: Bu ran, ci yi yu li suo jue er."
translations.idiomatic: Not so, this one prison official what decide."

sentences.zh: 是時郎中令石建為上別言兩人事。

translations.literal: At this time the Palace Secretary Shi Jian separately reported the matter of the two men to the Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: At the time, the Palace Secretary Shi Jian presented a separate report to the Emperor on the affair between the two men.

sentences.zh: 武安已罷朝，出止車門，召韓御史大夫載，怒曰：「與長孺共一老禿翁，何為首鼠兩端？」

translations.literal: Wu an yi ba chao, chu zhi che men, zhao Han yu shi da fu zai, nu yue: "Yu Chang ru gong yi lao tu weng, he wei shou shu liang duan?"
translations.idiomatic: Wuan already dismissed court, out stopped carriage gate, summoned Han imperial censor loaded cart, angered: "With Changru together one old bald old man, why head rat two ends?"

sentences.zh: 韓禦史良久謂丞相曰：「君何不自喜？

translations.literal: Han yu shi liang jiu wei cheng xiang yue: "Jun he bu zi xi?
translations.idiomatic: Han imperial censor long time told chancellor: "You why not self rejoice?

sentences.zh: 夫魏其毀君，君當免冠解印綬歸，曰『臣以肺腑幸得待罪，固非其任，魏其言皆是』。

translations.literal: Fu Wei qi hui jun, jun dang mian guan jie yin shou gui, yue "chen yi fei fu xing de dai zui, gu fei qi ren, Wei qi yan jie shi".
translations.idiomatic: Because Weiqi slanders you, you should remove hat loosen seal ribbon return, say 'subject with lung liver fortunate got serve crime, firmly not that duty, Weiqi words all correct.'

sentences.zh: 如此，上必多君有讓，不廢君。

translations.literal: If you do this, the Emperor will surely commend your magnanimity and not dismiss you.
translations.idiomatic: If you do so, the Emperor will surely commend your magnanimity and will not remove you from office.

sentences.zh: 魏其必內愧，杜門齰舌自殺。

translations.literal: Weiqi will surely feel inner shame, shut his door, bite his tongue, and kill himself.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi, consumed with shame, will shut his gates, bite his tongue in remorse, and take his own life.

sentences.zh: 今人毀君，君亦毀人，譬如賈豎女子爭言，何其無大體也！」

translations.literal: Jin ren hui jun, jun yi hui ren, pi ru Jia shu nu zi zheng yan, he qi wu da ti ye!"
translations.idiomatic: Now people slander you, you also slander people, like merchant slave girl contend speech, how without great propriety!

sentences.zh: 武安謝罪曰：「爭時急，不知出此。」

translations.literal: Wu an xie zui yue: "Zheng shi ji, bu zhi chu ci."
translations.idiomatic: Wuan apologized crime: "Contended time urgent, not know come out this."

sentences.zh: 於是上使禦史簿責魏其所言灌夫，頗不讎，欺謾。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Emperor sent the Imperial Censor to compile a record and question what Weiqi had said about Guan Fu. Much of it did not match the facts and was found deceitful and reckless.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon the Emperor dispatched the Imperial Censor to compile a formal record and examine what the Marquis of Weiqi had claimed about Guan Fu. Many of his statements did not match the facts and were found to be deceitful and reckless.

sentences.zh: 劾系都司空。

translations.literal: He was impeached and confined in the prison of the Director of Works.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi was impeached and confined in the prison of the Director of Works.

sentences.zh: 孝景時，魏其常受遺詔，曰「事有不便，以便宜論上」。

translations.literal: Xiao Jing shi, Wei qi chang shou yi zhao, yue "shi you bu bian, yi bian yi lun shang".
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing time, Weiqi often received posthumous edict, said "matters have not convenient, with discretion discuss emperor".

sentences.zh: 及系，灌夫罪至族，事日急，諸公莫敢複明言於上。

translations.literal: Once confined, Guan Fu's crimes were deemed to warrant clan extermination. The matter grew more urgent by the day, but none of the grandees dared to speak openly to the Emperor again.
translations.idiomatic: With both men in prison, Guan Fu's crimes were now judged to warrant the execution of his entire clan. The matter grew more urgent by the day, yet none of the grandees dared speak out to the Emperor on their behalf.

sentences.zh: 魏其乃使昆弟子上書言之，幸得複召見。

translations.literal: Weiqi then had a kinsman submit a memorial speaking on the matter and was fortunately granted another audience.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi then had a kinsman submit a memorial on the matter and was fortunate enough to be granted another audience.

sentences.zh: 書奏上，而案尚書大行無遺詔。

translations.literal: The memorial was presented to the Emperor, but upon examining the records of the Secretariat, no such posthumous edict could be found.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial was submitted to the throne, but when the records of the Imperial Secretariat were examined, no such posthumous edict was found on file.

sentences.zh: 詔書獨藏魏其家，家丞封。

translations.literal: The edict was found only in the Marquis of Weiqi's household, sealed by his household steward.
translations.idiomatic: The edict existed only in the Marquis of Weiqi's household, where it had been sealed away by his household steward.

sentences.zh: 乃劾魏其矯先帝詔，罪當棄市。

translations.literal: He was accordingly impeached for forging the former Emperor's edict, a crime punishable by public execution.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi was thereupon impeached for forging a decree of the former Emperor—a crime punishable by public execution.

sentences.zh: 五年十月，悉論灌夫及家屬。

translations.literal: In the tenth month of the fifth year, Guan Fu and all his family members were tried and sentenced.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of the fifth year, Guan Fu and all his family members were tried and sentenced to death.

sentences.zh: 魏其良久乃聞，聞即恚，病痱，不食欲死。

translations.literal: Weiqi did not learn of this for a long time. When he heard, he was immediately enraged, fell ill with paralysis, stopped eating and wished to die.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi did not learn of this for some time. When he finally heard the news, he was overcome with rage, fell ill with a paralytic condition, refused to eat, and wished only to die.

sentences.zh: 或聞上無意殺魏其，魏其複食，治病，議定不死矣。

translations.literal: Some heard the Emperor had no intention of killing Weiqi. Weiqi resumed eating, treated his illness, and it was discussed and settled that he would not die.
translations.idiomatic: Then it was rumored that the Emperor had no intention of executing the Marquis of Weiqi. Upon hearing this, the Marquis resumed eating and sought treatment for his illness, and it seemed settled that he would be spared.

sentences.zh: 乃有蜚語為惡言聞上，故以十二月晦論棄市渭城。

translations.literal: Then there were flying rumors making malicious words that reached the Emperor. Therefore, on the last day of the twelfth month, he was sentenced to public execution at Weicheng.
translations.idiomatic: But then malicious rumors and slanderous words reached the Emperor's ears. And so, on the last day of the twelfth month, the Marquis of Weiqi was sentenced to death and publicly executed at Weicheng.

sentences.zh: 其春，武安侯病，專呼服謝罪。

translations.literal: The following spring, the Marquis of Wu'an fell ill, constantly calling out to his attendants and confessing his sins.
translations.idiomatic: The following spring, the Marquis of Wu'an fell gravely ill. He was heard constantly crying out and confessing his sins.

sentences.zh: 使巫視鬼者視之，見魏其、灌夫共守，欲殺之。

translations.literal: A shaman who could see ghosts was brought to examine him. The shaman saw the spirits of Weiqi and Guan Fu standing guard over him, intent on killing him.
translations.idiomatic: A shaman with the ability to see ghosts was summoned to examine him. The shaman saw the spirits of the Marquis of Weiqi and Guan Fu standing guard over him, intent on taking his life.

sentences.zh: 竟死。

translations.literal: In the end he died.
translations.idiomatic: In the end, the Marquis of Wu'an died.

sentences.zh: 子恬嗣。

translations.literal: His son Tian succeeded him.
translations.idiomatic: His son Tian succeeded to the marquisate.

sentences.zh: 元朔三年，武安侯坐衣襜褕入宮，不敬。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yuanshuo, the Marquis of Wu'an was found guilty of entering the palace wearing informal robes, an act of disrespect.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of the Yuanshuo era, the new Marquis of Wu'an was charged with the offense of entering the palace in informal attire, an act of disrespect.

sentences.zh: 淮南王安謀反覺，治。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan, An, was discovered plotting rebellion, and an investigation was launched.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Huainan, Liu An, was discovered to have been plotting rebellion, an investigation was launched.

sentences.zh: 王前朝，武安侯為太尉，時迎王至霸上，謂王曰：「上未有太子，大王最賢，高祖孫，即宮車晏駕，非大王立當誰哉！」

translations.literal: Wang qian chao, Wu an hou wei tai wei, shi ying wang zhi Ba shang, wei wang yue: "Shang wei you tai zi, da wang zui xian, Gao zu sun, ji gong che yan jia, fei da wang li dang shui zai!"
translations.idiomatic: King before court, Wuan marquis as commander, at time welcomed king to Bashang, told king: "Emperor not yet have crown prince, great king most worthy, Gaozu grandson, if palace carriage evening drive, not great king establish should who!"

sentences.zh: 淮南王大喜，厚遺金財物。

translations.literal: The King of Huainan was greatly pleased and lavished him with gold, valuables, and gifts.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan was greatly pleased and lavished him with gold, valuables, and other gifts.

sentences.zh: 上自魏其時不直武安，特為太后故耳。

translations.literal: The Emperor from the time of Weiqi's affair did not consider Wu'an right, but indulged him solely on account of the Empress Dowager.
translations.idiomatic: From the time of the Marquis of Weiqi's affair, the Emperor had in truth considered the Marquis of Wu'an to be in the wrong, but had indulged him solely on account of the Empress Dowager.

sentences.zh: 及聞淮南王金事，上曰：「使武安侯在者，族矣。」

translations.literal: Ji wen Huai nan wang jin shi, shang yue: "Shi Wu an hou zai zhe, zu yi."
translations.idiomatic: When heard Huainan king gold matter, emperor said: "If Wuan marquis here, clan exterminated."

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：魏其、武安皆以外戚重，灌夫用一時決筴而名顯。

translations.literal: Tai shi gong yue: Wei qi, Wu an jie yi wai qi zhong, Guan fu yong yi shi jue mou er ming xian.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Scribe said: Weiqi, Wuan both with maternal relatives heavy, Guan Fu used one time decisive plan and name prominent.

sentences.zh: 魏其之舉以吳楚，武安之貴在日月之際。

translations.literal: Weiqi rose to prominence through Wu and Chu; Wu'an achieved nobility in the interval of a change of reign.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi rose to prominence through the rebellion of Wu and Chu, while the Marquis of Wu'an achieved his nobility in the brief interval between reigns.

sentences.zh: 然魏其誠不知時變，灌夫無術而不遜，兩人相翼，乃成禍亂。

translations.literal: But Weiqi truly not know time change, Guan Fu no method and not modest, two people mutually helped, then became calamity chaos.

sentences.zh: 武安負貴而好權，杯酒責望，陷彼兩賢。

translations.literal: Wuan bore nobility but loved power, goblet wine blamed disappointed, trapped those two worthy.

sentences.zh: 嗚呼哀哉！

translations.literal: Wu hu ai zai!
translations.idiomatic: Alas alas!

sentences.zh: 遷怒及人，命亦不延。

translations.literal: Transferred anger reached people, fate also not prolonged.

sentences.zh: 眾庶不載，竟被惡言。

translations.literal: Multitude people not carry, finally received evil words.

sentences.zh: 嗚呼哀哉！

translations.literal: Wu hu ai zai!
translations.idiomatic: Alas alas!

sentences.zh: 禍所從來矣！

translations.literal: Huo suo cong lai yi!
translations.idiomatic: Calamity what from came!
